title: podman restoring error
body:
After checkpointing at the source node I have copied those checkpointed files to the destination node and tried to restore them.
When I use the restore command the error pop up is:
`Error: did not receive systemd slice as cgroup parent when using systemd to manage cgroups: invalid argument
`
How to solve this problem? 

system using:
Ubuntu: 20.04
podman: 4.0.0-dev


Thank you.

comments.body: Please provide more information - a full reproducer and the complete output of `podman info` on both nodes. Where did you get your Podman build - are you building from source?

@mheon I have installed podman newly. At first, it was 4.0.0-dev.
Now I have installed 3.3.1 on both nodes.

I am getting the new error now.
Command uses:
```
$ sudo podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd
$ sudo podman ps
$ sudo podman container checkpoint <container_id> -e /tmp/checkpoint.tar.gz
$ sudo scp /tmp/checkpoint.tar.gz <destination_system>:/tmp
$sudo podman container restore -i /tmp/checkpoint.tar.gz
```

Checkpointing was ok but while I was trying to restore on the destination node I am getting below error:
`Error: error creating container storage: parsing named reference "d54056386fbb1ea69f9332f35ab083dd7062cc9cb78ed28ce6b8f85e9dfb56b3": invalid repository name (d54056386fbb1ea69f9332f35ab083dd7062cc9cb78ed28ce6b8f85e9dfb56b3), cannot specify 64-byte hexadecimal strings`


System using:
_Distributor ID:	Ubuntu
Description:	Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS
Release:	20.04
Codename:	focal_

Docker: _Docker version 20.10.9, build c2ea9bc_

Podman info:

i think you might have to do `sudo podman pull docker.io/library/httpd` on the target system before importing. I've seen this error message when the target host has an outdated/different version of the image compared to the source host.

Yes, that was the problem. 
Thank you.

Would you mind please, have a look at the errors
```
(00.032990) cg: Restore special props
(00.033007) cg: Restoring cgroup property value [0-3] to [cpuset/machine.slice/libpod-f7ff7ce4bd07c9d203412628370134e201ae6f24a0f8971d27306113c436cecb.scope/cpuset.cpus]
(00.033060) Error (criu/cgroup.c:1350): cg: Failed writing 0-3 to cpuset/machine.slice/libpod-f7ff7ce4bd07c9d203412628370134e201ae6f24a0f8971d27306113c436cecb.scope/cpuset.cpus: Permission denied
(00.033079) Error (criu/cgroup.c:1617): cg: Restoring cpuset.cpus special property failed
(00.033081) Error (criu/cgroup.c:1677): cg: Restoring special cpuset props failed!
```

How to solve this one? 
Thank you.

Hello.

How to collect dump size, dumping time, restoring size, restoring time using podman?

Like CRIU has some command to collect this thing. Can I collect these stats using podman? Because I need these stats for my work.
We do not directly support these at present. Feel free to open an RFE to add them.
How to open an RFE?
A new Github issue tagged as "kind/enhancement" should do.
So I can open a new issue that will be okay right?
Yes
For the time being, it is not possible to collect these stats, right?
Not directly from Podman. It may be possible to query criu directly to retrieve them.
As I am using podman for checkpointing and restoring a container, is it possible to use criu to collect these stats?

Does podman support diskless migration? 

If support, then how to do that? 

Also, how to collect timestamps/time with resource usage like when I start dump/restore, I need to collect that time. After a short duration, maybe a few nano-second periods, it will collect time and resource usage.

@mheon Will you, please have a look at this?
@adrianreber PTAL
The original issue is solved so this ticket should be closed. The rest is very much general questions and off topic and should go to a mailing list or forum IMHO

@adrianreber 
> Yes, use `--ignore-rootfs` when exporting the checkpoint.

Thank you.
Is it the same as https://criu.org/Disk-less_migration this one? 

Sorry for reopening it again. It might be the last question for this issue.

> > Yes, use `--ignore-rootfs` when exporting the checkpoint.
> 
> Thank you. Is it the same as https://criu.org/Disk-less_migration this one?

No. That is something completely different and not implemented, as far as I know, by any container runtime or container engine. Patches are always welcome.
